archetype_reasoning: The dominant pattern in recent resolved trades is mass near-certainty buying of YES on 'Will Trump visit China by March 31?' at prices of 0.012-0.014 — classic yield harvesting on near-certain NO outcomes (which correctly resolved NO, meaning these YES bets lost or were near-zero recoveries). Wait — the YES trades at 0.012-0.014 WON, meaning the market resolved YES despite low probability implied at entry. However, the open positions show consistent NO-side bets at prices like 0.94 and 0.87 (equivalent to buying NO cheaply), plus 'Iran Strike on Israel' YES at 0.965 — these are near-certainty premium collection plays. The trader systematically enters positions at near-certain prices (sub-0.05 for YES or above 0.90 for NO) to harvest small premiums, with the one genuine directional call at 0.37 being a loss.
domain_blindspots: Trump_US: accuracy inflated by near-certainty near-zero YES buys that happened to win; one directional bet at 0.37 was lost
Iran_ME: small sample, open positions concentrated in near-certainty NO side

The geo_elo_active of 2128 is below the LEGENDARY threshold and the raw ELO is inflated by volume of near-certain wins. The one genuine contested-price call lost. Signal value for STR-003 is minimal as trades don't reflect informational edge.

summary: This trader's impressive win rate and ELO are almost entirely products of near-certainty position harvesting at prices below 0.02 or above 0.93, not genuine forecasting. The record across 34 trades spans only 3 markets, and the sole contested-price directional call (Trump China visit at 0.37) was a loss. Open positions continue the same pattern: collecting small premiums on near-certain outcomes in Iran_ME. Until this trader demonstrates consistent edge at contested prices across diverse markets, their signals should be discounted heavily for STR-003 purposes.
